memoryArtifacts.headline: A golden bust of Frederick I, given to his godfather Count Otto of Cappenberg in 1171. It was used as a reliquary in Cappenberg Abbey and is said in the deed of the gift to have been made "in the likeness of the emperor".

A golden bust of Frederick I, given to his godfather Count Otto of Cappenberg in 1171. It was used as a reliquary in Cappenberg Abbey and is said in the deed of the gift to have been made "in the likeness of the emperor".

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Friedrich I "Barbarossa"
Holy Roman Emperor. Born in Waiblingen, Germany, Barbarossa was elected King of Germany on March 4th, 1152, succeeding his uncle, Conrad III, and he was further crowned Holy Roman Emperor in 1155. As the son of Frederick of Hohenstaufen, duke of Swabia, and Judith of Bavaria, of the rival Guelph dynasty, Frederick was descended from Germany's two principal families, making him an acceptable choice for the Empire's princely electors as heir to the Imperial crown. Barbarossa undertook six expeditions into Italy, in the first of which he was crowned emperor in Rome by Pope Adrian IV. Thereafter, relations between Emperor and Pope descended into bitter conflict culminating in Frederick's defeat at the Battle of Legnano near Milan (1176) by the pro-Papal Lombard League. After making his peace with Pope Alexander III, Frederick embarked on the Third Crusade (1189) with Philip Augustus of France and Richard I of England; as he and his men had marched through the hot desert, the sight of water so overjoyed him that he jumped into the Saleph river in Cilicia in south-eastern Anatolia, without removing his armor, and drowned. His surviving knights were so distraught that some comitted suicide; others joined the Saracens, convinced that God himself had abandoned them. Some of the surviving knights put Barbarossa's body in a pickle barrel, and planned to take it to Jerusalem, as a small form of victory, but the stench became too foul, and Barbarossa was buried at St Peter's Cathedral in Antioch, the site where the Lance of Longinus had been discovered. The Cathedral was, in later years, burned to the ground. However, Barbarossa is the subject of a sleeping hero legend. He is said not to be dead, but asleep with his knights in a cave in Kyffhäuser mountain in Thuringia, Germany, and that when ravens should cease to fly around the mountain, he would awake and restore Germany to its ancient greatness. According to the story his red beard has grown through the table beside which he sits. His eyes are half closed in sleep, but now and then he raises his hand and sends a boy out to see if the ravens have stopped flying.

Friedrich I, 'Barbarossa' Emperor Of The Holy Roman Empire called Barbaros sa or Red Beard, succeeded his uncle Conrad III as king of Germany in 115 2. He became Holy Roman Emperor in 1155. The German people admired and res pected him as a great national hero. In 1180, he defeated his great riv al for power in Germany, Henry the Lion, Duke of Saxony and Bavaria. Wi th the help of loyal princes and an able administration, he enforced his a uthority in Germany and the Slavic borderlands to the east.

He was less successful in a bitter struggle against Pope Alexander III a nd the Lombard League of North Italian cities. The League defeated Frederi ck at the Battle of Legnano in 1176. It was in this battle that foot soldi ers recorded their first great victory over feudal cavalry. The Lombard ci ties forced Frederick to grant them self-government in the Peace of Consta nce in 1183. The Emperor
started on the Third Crusade to the Holy Land in 1189, but drowned the ne xt year while crossing a river. A German legend, however, says that Barbar ossa never really died but is sleeping beside a huge table in the Kyffhaus er Mountains. When his beard grows completely around the table, the lege nd says, he will arise and conquer Germany's enemies.

Frederick I (Holy Roman Empire), called Frederick Barbarossa (1123?-90), H oly Roman emperor and king of Germany (1152-90), king of Italy (1155-90 ), and as Frederick III, duke of Swabia (1147-52, 1167-68). He was born in Waiblingen, the son of Frederick II of Hohenstaufen, duke of Swab ia (1090-1147), and the nephew of Conrad III, king of Germany. Conrad II I, favoring Frederick over his own son, on his deathbed recommended to t he German princes that Frederick be chosen for the German kingship and t he imperial throne. Accordingly, after the death of his uncle in 1152, Fre derick Barbarossa was made German king and elected Holy Roman empero r. He conceived of his imperial title as a grant from God, through the Ger man princes, and wished to restore the glory of the Roman Empire. He conse quently decided to consolidate the imperial position
in Germany and Italy and began by issuing a general order for peace amo ng the princes of Germany, at the same time granting them extensive conces sions. In 1154 he proceeded to Italy, where he received the Lombard cro wn at Pavia. The following year he was crowned Holy Roman emperor by
Pope Adrian IV, whose authority Frederick had reinstated before his corona tion.

In 1156 Pope Adrian aroused Frederick against the papacy by implyi ng in a letter to him that the emperor held lands only as a fief from t he pope. Two years later Frederick incurred the hostility of the Lombar ds by demanding recognition of all his royal rights, including his pow er to appoint the imperial podesta, or governor, in every town. Such citi es as Milan, Piacenza, Brescia, and Crema considered that demand a deni al of their communal liberties and in 1158 began a struggle that lasted un til 1183 and required Frederick to lead five expeditions to Italy. Betwe en 1158 and 1162 Frederick warred with Milan and its allies, subduing th at city and confirming claims to other Italian cities. Meanwhile Frederi ck had set up a series of antipopes in opposition to the reigning pope, Al exander III, who espoused the cause of the Milanese and their allies and w ho, in 1165, excommunicated Frederick. By attacking the Leonine City in Ro me in 1167-68, Frederick was able to install one of the antipopes, Pasch al III (died 1168), on the papal throne. The Lombard League, consisti ng of the cities of Milan, Parma, Padua, Verona, Piacenza, Bologna, Cremon a, Mantua, Bergamo, and Brescia, was formed in 1167 and eventually acknowl edged Pope Alexander as leader. During the next seven years the league acq uired military strength, rebuilt Milan, constructed the fortress city of A lessandria, and organized a federal system of administration. The fifth ex pedition (1174-76) of Frederick to Italy terminated in defeat by the Lomba rd League at Legnano. The defeat was significant in military history, beca use it was the first major triumph of infantry over a mounted army of feud al knights. Frederick was forced in 1177 to acknowledge Alexander III as p ope and in
1183 to sign the Peace of Constance, acceding to the demands of the Lombar ds for autonomy but retaining imperial suzerainty over the towns.

Although imperial control in Italy was virtually ended by his defeat at Le gnano, Frederick managed to enhance his prestige in central Europe. He ma de Poland tributary to the empire, raised Bohemia to the rank of a kingdo m, and erected the margravate of Austria into an independent hereditary du chy. His own power as emperor in Germany was firmly established in 1180, w hen he ended his
long struggle with the Welfs by putting down a revolt led by the Welf Hen ry the Lion and depriving him of most of his lands.

Frederick initiated the Third Crusade in 1189, and in the next year, havi ng resigned the government of the empire to his son Henry, later Holy Rom an Emperor Henry VI, set out for Asia Minor. After gaining two great victo ries over the Muslims at Philomelion (now Ak3ehir) and Iconium (now Konya ), he was drowned in the Calycadnus (now Goksu) River in Cilicia (now in T urkey) on June 10, 1190.

Source: 'The World Book Encyclopedia', 1968, p F422. 'Frederick I (Holy Ro man Empire),'

zh.lifeSummary: 當Friedrich I. 'Barbarossa' römisch-deutscher Kaiser於1122年12月出生在德國艾爾薩斯-洛斯林根哈瑙時，他的父親Friedrich II. 'der Einäugige' Herzog von Schwaben32歲，母親Judith von Bayern22歲。他於1147年與Adela von Vohburg在英國蘇格蘭諾斯 艾斯爾霍利結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1190年6月10日在土耳其奧爾度易吉斯格可素享年67歲，埋葬在土耳其哈代安塔吉亞安塔吉亞。

nameMeanings.name: Friedrich
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: from frid ‘peace’ + rīc ‘power’; a royal and imperial name from an early date.

variants.name: Frederick
meanings: From an Old French name of Germanic origin, from fred, frid ‘peace’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced into Britain by them, but did not survive long. Modern use in Britain dates from the 17th century, and it became more frequent in the 18th among followers of the Elector of Hanover, who in 1714 became George I of England. It was reinforced by the vogue for Germanic names in Victorian times.

variants.name: Friederich
meanings: from frid ‘peace’ + rīc ‘power’; a royal and imperial name from an early date.

en.lifeSummary: When Friedrich I. 'Barbarossa' römisch-deutscher Kaiser was born in December 1122, in Haguenau, Alsace-Lorraine, Germany, his father, Friedrich II. 'der Einäugige' Herzog von Schwaben, was 32 and his mother, Judith von Bayern, was 22. He married Adela von Vohburg in 1147, in Holy, North Ayrshire, Scotland, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died on 10 June 1190, in Göksu, İkizce, Ordu, Türkiye, at the age of 67, and was buried in Antakya, Antakya, Hatay, Türkiye.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Friedrich I. 'Barbarossa' römisch-deutscher Kaiser est né en décembre 1122, à Haguenau, Alsace-Lorraine, Allemagne, son père, Friedrich II. 'der Einäugige' Herzog von Schwaben, avait 32 ans et sa mère, Judith von Bayern, avait 22 ans. Il a épousé Adela von Vohburg en 1147, à Holy, North Ayrshire, Écosse, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé le 10 juin 1190, à Göksu, İkizce, Ordu, Turquie, à l’âge de 67 ans, et a été enterré à Antakya, Antakya, Hatay, Turquie.

de.lifeSummary: Als Friedrich I. 'Barbarossa' römisch-deutscher Kaiser im Dezember 1122 in Hagenau, Elsaß-Lothringen, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Friedrich II. 'der Einäugige' Herzog von Schwaben 32 und seine Mutter Judith von Bayern 22. Er hat 1147 in Holy, North Ayrshire, Schottland, United Kingdom Adela von Vohburg geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb am 10. Juni 1190 im Alter von 67 Jahren in Göksu, İkizce, Ordu, Türkei und wurde in Antakya, Antakya, Hatay, Türkei begraben.

it.Vohburg, Nordgau, Holy Roman Emplire: Holy, North Ayrshire, Scozia, Regno Unito
it.lifeSummary: Quando Friedrich I. 'Barbarossa' römisch-deutscher Kaiser è nato il nel dicembre 1122 nel Haguenau, Elsaß-Lothringen, Germania, il suo padre, Friedrich II. 'der Einäugige' Herzog von Schwaben, aveva 32 anni e il suo madre, Judith von Bayern, aveva 22 anni. Lui sposato Adela von Vohburg nel 1147, nel Holy, North Ayrshire, Scozia, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì il 10 giugno 1190, nel Göksu, İkizce, Ordu, Turchia, all'età di 67 anni, e fu sepolto a Antakya, Antakya, Hatay, Turchia.

ja.lifeSummary: Friedrich I. 'Barbarossa' römisch-deutscher Kaiserが1122年12月にドイツエルサss-ロスリンジェンハグエナウで生まれた時，父Friedrich II. 'der Einäugige' Herzog von Schwabenは32歳，母Judith von Bayernは22歳であった。1147年にイギリススコットランドノース エイルシュアイアホーリーでAdela von Vohburgと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。彼は1190年6月10日にトルコオルドウイキズスグoクスーで67歳で死去し，トルコハティイアンタクヤアンタクヤにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Friedrich I. 'Barbarossa' römisch-deutscher Kaiser(이)가 1122년 12월 독일 엘사ss-로스링 하구나에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Friedrich II. 'der Einäugige' Herzog von Schwaben(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Judith von Bayern(은)는 22세였다. 그는 1147년 영국 스코틀랜드 노르스 에이르실르 홀이에서 Adela von Vohburg(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1190년 6월 10일 튀르키예 르드 이키즈스 그o크수에서 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 튀르키예 하타이 앤타키아 앤타키아에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Friedrich I. 'Barbarossa' römisch-deutscher Kaiser nasceu em dezembro de 1122, em Haguenau, Elsaß-Lothringen, Alemanha, seu pai, Friedrich II. 'der Einäugige' Herzog von Schwaben, tinha 32 anos e sua mãe, Judith von Bayern, tinha 22 anos. Ele casou-se com Adela von Vohburg em 1147, em Holy, North Ayrshire, Escócia, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 10 de junho de 1190, em Göksu, İkizce, Ordu, Turquia, com 67 anos, e foi sepultado em Antakya, Antakya, Hatay, Turquia.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Friedrich I. 'Barbarossa' römisch-deutscher Kaiser родился декабрь 1122 г. в местности хагуенау, элсаss-лотринген, Германия, его отцу по имени Friedrich II. 'der Einäugige' Herzog von Schwaben было 32 года, а матери, которую звали Judith von Bayern, 22. Он женился на женщине по имени Adela von Vohburg в 1147 г. в местности холи, норт аиршир, Шотландия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер 10 июня 1190 г. в местности гoкси, икизс, орду, Турция в возрасте 67 лет и был похоронен в местности антакя, антакя, хатеи, Турция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Friedrich I. 'Barbarossa' römisch-deutscher Kaiser nació en diciembre de 1122, en Haguenau, Elsaß-Lothringen, Alemania, su papá, Friedrich II. 'der Einäugige' Herzog von Schwaben, tenía 32 años y su mamá, Judith von Bayern, tenía 22 años. Se casó con Adela von Vohburg en 1147, en Holy, North Ayrshire, Escocia, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió el 10 de junio de 1190, en Göksu, İkizce, Ordu, Turquía, a la edad de 67 años, y fue sepultado en Antakya, Antakya, Hatay, Turquía.
